CONTENT: 1️⃣ Review the Pre-Qualified Shortlist
Open the template README and read through the curated shortlist of target Awesome Lists
Verify each shortlisted list is still actively maintained — check last commit date
Verify each list's current star count and note whether it has grown or declined
Review each list's CONTRIBUTING.md for any submission rules added since this template was created
Confirm this repository still meets the quality bar for each list (README quality
Remove any lists from your working shortlist that this repo no longer qualifies for
Add any newly discovered relevant lists to your working shortlist with rationale
2️⃣ Draft Your Submission Message
Open the submission message template from the README
Personalise the one-line repo description for the specific audience of each target list
Confirm the GitHub repo URL is correct and publicly accessible
Format your entry as a Markdown link matching the target list's existing style
Cross-check your entry against nearby entries in the target list for tone and length consistency
Prepare the reusable PR description — adapt for each list as needed
3️⃣ Submit to Each Shortlisted List
Fork the first target awesome list repo
Create a submission branch (e.g. add-todoist-playbook)
Add your entry in the appropriate section of the list README
Open a pull request using the reusable PR description from Section 2
Record the PR URL and submission date in the tracking table in the README
Repeat for each remaining list in the shortlist
4️⃣ Track & Follow Up
Review all open PRs after 7 days and respond to any maintainer comments
Update the tracking table status for any merged or closed PRs
If a PR is rejected — note the reason and decide whether to revise the entry or move on
Follow up with a polite note on any PR that has been open for 14+ days with no response
After 30 days — review the tracking table and close out any stale submissions
Verify that any merged listings display correctly on the live awesome list
